None of these measure magnitude-stratified bias directly; the logical chain from observed global gains (~0.8–0.9 pp accuracy, ~1–1.2 F1) to the headline bias-reduction claim is unsupported within the section and requires close consistency scrutiny.","R1.3":"Load-bearing: the primary quantitative claim ('reduces magnitude-dependent classification bias by approximately 35%') appears in both the opening and closing paragraphs, yet the entire evidence block presents only aggregate classification metrics that cannot themselves establish a 35% bias reduction. rationale.R1.2: The concluding sentences assert '35% reduction in magnitude-dependent bias' and 'substantially reduces… bias', but the evidence block reports only global metrics (accuracy, macro-F1, ROC-AUC). None of these measure magnitude-stratified bias directly; the logical chain from observed global gains (~0.8–0.9 pp accuracy, ~1–1.2 F1) to the headline bias-reduction claim is unsupported within the section and requires close consistency scrutiny.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the primary quantitative claim ('reduces magnitude-dependent classification bias by approximately 35%') appears in both the opening and closing paragraphs, yet the entire evidence block presents only aggregate classification metrics that cannot themselves establish a 35% bias reduction. The bias-metric results (e.g., per-magnitude-bin performance curves, bias coefficients) that would directly support this claim are absent, making the evidence-to-claim alignment the single most critical failure mode for this section.
